title: St. Joseph's Catholic Church
tagline: St. Joseph's Catholic Church — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

heroImage.alt: St. Joseph's Catholic Church, Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: St. Joseph's Catholic Church is a much-loved spiritual town in Jammu & Kashmir, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Joseph's Catholic Church is a parish of the Roman Catholic Church in Baramulla, Jammu and Kashmir, India. It was established in 1891 by the Mill Hill Missionaries, making it the oldest Catholic church in Jammu and Kashmir, and currently belongs to the Jammu Srinagar Diocese. St.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Baramulla (1814)
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Baramulla was an engagement fought in 1814 during Ranjit Singh's expedition toward Kashmir. The action formed part of Ram Dayal's advance through the Bahramgala route and the Pir Panjal into the Kashmir Valley.

topPlaces.name: Baramulla
topPlaces.description: Baramulla (Urdu pronunciation: [bɑːɾɑːmuːlɑː]), also known as Varmul (Kashmiri pronunciation: [ʋarmul]) in Kashmiri, is a city and municipality of the Baramulla district of the Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the disputed Kashmir region. It is also the administrative headquarters of the Baramulla district, located on the banks of the River Jhelum downstream from Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Rohama
topPlaces.description: Rohama or Rohoam is one of the largest villages in the Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. This village is situated 12 km from district headquarter 'It shares its boundaries with 21 villages' there are more than 40 government and semi-government offices, it got tehsil status in 2005 and consists of many sub district offices.

topPlaces.name: Fatehgadh, Baramulla
topPlaces.description: Fatehgadh (locally known as Fatehgarh) is a village located in Narvaw Baramulla tehsil of Baramulla district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Fatehgadh village has 2537 population of which 1302 are males and 1235 are females according to India Census 2011.

topPlaces.name: Sheeri, Baramulla
topPlaces.description: Sheeri is a village located in Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The village has two adjoining areas namely "Sheeri Bala" and "Sheeri Payeen".

topPlaces.name: Chakloo
topPlaces.description: Chakloo, also known as Chakla, is a village on the bank of the Jhelum River, in the Baramulla District of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Ladoora
topPlaces.description: Ladoora is a notified area and village in the Baramulla district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is located on the banks of River Jhelum.

topPlaces.name: Wagoora
topPlaces.description: Wagoora or Wogur is a tehsil and a small town in Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

items.note: The Battle of Baramulla was an engagement fought in 1814 during Ranjit Singh's expedition toward Kashmir. The action formed part of Ram Dayal's advance through the Bahramgala route and the Pir Panjal into the Kashmir Valley.

Baramulla (Urdu pronunciation: [bɑːɾɑːmuːlɑː]), also known as Varmul (Kashmiri pronunciation: [ʋarmul]) in Kashmiri, is a city and municipality of the Baramulla district of the Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the disputed Kashmir region. It is also the administrative headquarters of the Baramulla district, located on the banks of the River Jhelum downstream from Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir.

Rohama or Rohoam is one of the largest villages in the Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. This village is situated 12 km from district headquarter 'It shares its boundaries with 21 villages' there are more than 40 government and semi-government offices, it got tehsil status in 2005 and consists of many sub district offices.

Fatehgadh (locally known as Fatehgarh) is a village located in Narvaw Baramulla tehsil of Baramulla district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Fatehgadh village has 2537 population of which 1302 are males and 1235 are females according to India Census 2011.
Sheeri is a village located in Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The village has two adjoining areas namely "Sheeri Bala" and "Sheeri Payeen".
Chakloo, also known as Chakla, is a village on the bank of the Jhelum River, in the Baramulla District of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
Ladoora is a notified area and village in the Baramulla district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is located on the banks of River Jhelum.
Wagoora or Wogur is a tehsil and a small town in Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Joseph's Catholic Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Joseph's Catholic Church is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Joseph's Catholic Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (146 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Joseph's Catholic Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Joseph's Catholic Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Joseph's Catholic Church?
faq.a: Stays in St. Joseph's Catholic Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Joseph's Catholic Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Baramulla (1814), Baramulla, Rohama, Fatehgadh, Baramulla, Sheeri, Baramulla.
